RADAR ESTIMATED THAT 0.5 TO 1.2 INCHES OF RAIN FELL OVER THE CEDAR WILDFIRE BURN SCAR. EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED 12 TO 18 INCH FLOOD CREST MOVED THROUGH THE CEDAR CREEK WASH THROUGH THE TOWN OF CEDAR CREEK BETWEEN 615 PM AND 645 PM.

RADAR ESTIMATED THAT 0.5 TO 1.2 INCHES OF RAIN FELL OVER THE CEDAR WILDFIRE BURN SCAR. EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED 12 TO 18 INCH FLOOD CREST MOVED THROUGH THE CEDAR CREEK WASH THROUGH THE TOWN OF CEDAR CREEK BETWEEN 615 PM AND 645 PM. NO HOMES WERE AFFECTED AND NO IMPACTS REPORTED.

RADAR ESTIMATED 1.5 TO 2.5 INCHES OF HEAVY RAIN OVER CENTRAL SECTIONS OF THE CEDAR WILDFIRE BURN SCAR STARTING AROUND 0530 PM. 4 TO 5 FOOT FLOOD CREST REACHED THE BRIDGE AT THE TOWN OF CEDAR CREEK AT 0720 PM. THE FLOOD CREST TOOK 1 HOUR AND 50 MINUTES TO COME DOWN OFF THE FIRE AND REACH THE TOWN OF CEDAR CREEK.

ESTIMATED PARIA RIVER RISE NEAR THE AZ/UT BORDER GREATER THAN 2 FEET IN 15-30 MINUTES. USGS PARIA RIVER GAGE AT HWY 89 IN UTAH REPORTED ~ 1.4 FT. RISE IN 15 MINUTES AT 6PM MST. USGS PARIA RIVER GAGE AT LEES FERRY REPORTED ~ 1.1 FT. RISE IN 30 MINUTES AT 9:45PM MST. EXTRAPOLATING THE FLOOD WAVE VELOCITY AND THE MUCH NARROWER CHANNEL NEAR THE AZ/UT BORDER, IT CAN BE INFERRED A RISE OF GREATER THAN 2 FEET WAS OBSERVED ALONG THE PARIA RIVER NEAR THE AZ/UT BORDER.
DEBRIS FLOW WAS REPORTED ACROSS HWY 191 AT MM 389-390... ROAD WAS TEMPORARILY CLOSED. RADAR ESTIMATED 1 INCH OF RAIN IN LESS THAN 60 MINUTES.
